Peserta akan mendapatkan pembekalan menyeluruh melalui pelatihan intensif dan rotasi kerja di berbagai unit, seperti konstruksi, keuangan dan akuntansi, sumber daya manusia, audit, pemasaran, administrasi proyek, hukum, teknologi informasi, hingga pengelolaan proyek. Tanggung jawab peserta mencakup membantu koordinasi proyek, melakukan analisis data dan penyusunan laporan, mendukung pengelolaan administrasi, mempelajari kebutuhan pelanggan, serta berkontribusi dalam upaya peningkatan efektivitas proses kerja. Peserta juga akan bekerja sama dengan para manajer dan tim lintas divisi untuk mengembangkan solusi dan strategi yang dapat meningkatkan efisiensi, produktivitas, serta kinerja perusahaan secara keseluruhan. Selain mendapatkan pengalaman kerja praktis, peserta juga diwajibkan aktif mengikuti berbagai sesi pelatihan, pengembangan kompetensi, dan kegiatan kepemimpinan yang telah dirancang perusahaan. Listed more than two days ago Talent Acquisition Associate di Everlash Ini adalah lowongan kerja Full time Jakarta Selatan , Jakarta Raya Enjoy free beauty treatments — including nails, lash, brow, and wax Develop your skills and grow with continuous learning opportunities Work in a friendly, inspiring, and supportive environment Enjoy free beauty treatments — including nails, lash, brow, and wax Develop your skills and grow with continuous learning opportunities Work in a friendly, inspiring, and supportive environment Your HR journey starts here. Source, screen & present top talent for our operations team. Fresh grads welcome, grow with us. Your HR journey starts here. Source, screen & present top talent for our operations team. Fresh grads welcome, grow with us. subClassification: Perekrutan – Internal Perekrutan – Internal classification: Sumber Daya Manusia & Perekrutan (Sumber Daya Manusia & Perekrutan) 2 hari yang lalu 2 hari yang lalu About the Role Are you a fresh graduate excited to launch your career in Human Resources? Do you enjoy meeting new people, building connections, and helping teams grow? We are looking for a Talent Acquisition Associate to join our Operations team! In this role, you will be the first point of contact for candidates joining our company. You will help us source, screen, and interview great people across our operational teams (GRO/Customer Service, Beautician, Store Leader, and more), and present the best ones to our hiring managers for selection. You will report directly to the Operational Manager, while also being part of our HR team. It is a fantastic opportunity to learn recruitment from day one, work closely with senior leaders, and grow into a future HR or People Operations professional. No recruitment experience? No problem! We will train you. What we care about most is your energy, curiosity, and willingness to learn. What You Will Do End-to-End Recruitment Source candidates through job portals (Jobstreet, LinkedIn), referrals, walk-ins, and job fairs. Screen CVs and conduct first-round interviews to assess candidate fit. Coordinate interview schedules between candidates and hiring managers. Present shortlisted candidates to the Operational Manager for selection. Recruitment Tracker & Reporting Maintain and update the Recruitment Tracker daily — every candidate, every stage, every status. This is a core part of the role. Prepare weekly recruitment reports: fulfilment rate, time-to-fill, sourcing channel performance, and pipeline health. Keep the candidate database clean, organized, and ready for the team to reference at any time. Candidate Pipeline & Sourcing Build and grow an active talent pipeline so we always have great candidates ready when business needs them. Explore creative sourcing channels — social media, communities, campus networks, and beyond. Provide a warm and professional candidate experience throughout the hiring journey. Handover & Coordination Work closely with the Operational Manager to understand hiring needs and timelines. Hand over endorsed candidates smoothly to the People Operations team for offering and onboarding. Flag blockers or changes early so the team can move quickly. Job Posting & Employer Branding Post job openings on Jobstreet, LinkedIn, Karir.com, and other relevant platforms. Help keep our employer branding fresh, friendly, and aligned with our company image. Track applicant numbers per posting and suggest improvements. Who We Are Looking For Education Fresh graduates or final-year students (D3/S1) from Psychology, Human Resource Management, Management, Communications, or related majors are encouraged to apply. Internship experience in HR, Recruitment, or People Operations is a plus — but not required. Skills & Mindset Eager to learn: you are curious, ask great questions, and pick things up quickly. People person: you enjoy talking to candidates and can build rapport easily. Organized & detail-oriented: you keep trackers up to date and never let candidates slip through the cracks. Driven & resilient: you can handle volume, deadlines, and a fast-paced environment. Communicative: you can write professional messages and follow up clearly with candidates and Users. Adaptive: comfortable when priorities or hiring targets shift. Technical Knowledge Comfortable with Microsoft Excel (basic to intermediate) and Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets). Familiar with AI tools and able to use AI for real work — drafting job ads, screening notes, candidate communications, and speeding up daily tasks. Familiar with social media and digital platforms; bonus if you have used LinkedIn, Jobstreet, or Karir.com before. Basic understanding of Indonesian Labor Law (UU Ketenagakerjaan) is a plus — we will train you. WhatsApp Business knowledge is a bonus for high-volume candidate communication. Languages Bahasa Indonesia (fluent / native). Basic English — enough to read job descriptions and write simple messages. Other Able to stay calm and focused during peak hiring seasons. What You Will Get A structured learning path — you will learn recruitment end-to-end from sourcing to handover. Mentorship from experienced Operations and People Operations leaders. Exposure to high-volume, real-world hiring across multiple operational roles. A clear career path into Recruitment Specialist, HR Generalist, or People Operations roles. A collaborative team that values speed, ownership, and kindness.

Prepare weekly recruitment reports: fulfilment rate, time-to-fill, sourcing channel performance, and pipeline health. Keep the candidate database clean, organized, and ready for the team to reference at any time. Candidate Pipeline & Sourcing Build and grow an active talent pipeline so we always have great candidates ready when business needs them. Explore creative sourcing channels — social media, communities, campus networks, and beyond. Provide a warm and professional candidate experience throughout the hiring journey. Handover & Coordination Work closely with the Operational Manager to understand hiring needs and timelines. Hand over endorsed candidates smoothly to the People Operations team for offering and onboarding. Flag blockers or changes early so the team can move quickly. Job Posting & Employer Branding Post job openings on Jobstreet, LinkedIn, Karir.com, and other relevant platforms. Help keep our employer branding fresh, friendly, and aligned with our company image. Track applicant numbers per posting and suggest improvements. People person: you enjoy talking to candidates and can build rapport easily. Organized & detail-oriented: you keep trackers up to date and never let candidates slip through the cracks. Driven & resilient: you can handle volume, deadlines, and a fast-paced environment. Communicative: you can write professional messages and follow up clearly with candidates and Users. Adaptive: comfortable when priorities or hiring targets shift. Technical Knowledge Comfortable with Microsoft Excel (basic to intermediate) and Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets). Familiar with AI tools and able to use AI for real work — drafting job ads, screening notes, candidate communications, and speeding up daily tasks. Familiar with social media and digital platforms; bonus if you have used LinkedIn, Jobstreet, or Karir.com before. Basic understanding of Indonesian Labor Law (UU Ketenagakerjaan) is a plus — we will train you. WhatsApp Business knowledge is a bonus for high-volume candidate communication. Languages Bahasa Indonesia (fluent / native). Basic English — enough to read job descriptions and write simple messages. Other Able to stay calm and focused during peak hiring seasons. A collaborative team that values speed, ownership, and kindness.

If you are a high-achieving graduate ready to push boundaries and work at the intersection of innovation and sustainability, your journey starts here! Our 12month Management Trainee Program in Operations and Project Management is designed to immerse you in the heart of our business. You won’t be watching from the sidelines—you will take on real responsibility from Day 1. Real-World Impact: Tackle high-stakes projects under the guidance of senior business leaders. Cross-Functional Rotations: Gain a 360 degree view of the industrial gas industry through diverse unit rotations. Elite Mentorship: Be paired with a senior executive who will act as your reporting officer and mentor to accelerate your professional growth. Are You Our Next Leader? We are looking for self-driven, resilient, and resourceful individuals who meet the following criteria: Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering (Chemical, Industrial, Electrical, Mechanical, or Civil). Fresh graduates (Class of 2025 or 2026) or professionals with maximum 1-year of full-time work experience. A minimum CGPA of 3.50 and above (out of 4.00 or equivalent). Strong leadership potential, sharp numerical skills, and excellent communication abilities. Proficiency in additional languages (such as Mandarin) will be a significant advantage. Flexibility to be based at our operational sites in the Cilegon or Bekasi (Cibitung or Cikarang) areas. The Roadmap to Joining Us The selection process is fast-paced and high-engagement. Please ensure you are available for the following dates: Stage 1: Application Start To kick off your application, please submit your details via the Google Form . If you are shortlisted, you will be invited for an on-site interview at Universitas Indonesia on 06 July 2026. 👉 Google Form Link: https://forms.gle/jZvbDvnDxVBdgWtv5 ⏳ Application Deadline: 12 June 2026 Stage 2: On-site Interview & Engagement at Universitas Indonesia Join us at Universitas Indonesia for an immersive on-site interview experience. This is your chance to connect with Air Liquide through interactive stakeholder sessions, collaborative focus group discussions, and personal interviews. Mark your calendar for the target event date on 06 July 2026. Please be advised that travel and transportation expenses to the campus venue are to be covered by the candidates. Stage 3: Final Panel Interview at Air Liquide Indonesia Office Join us at the Air Liquide Indonesia Office in West Jakarta for a final panel interview, tentatively scheduled for 13 July 2026 . This stage allows us to further explore our mutual compatibility. Please be aware that applicants are responsible for arranging and funding their own travel to the office. Stage 4: Offer and Onboarding Upon completing Stage 3, successful applicants will receive an offer to join the Management Trainee Program, which is expected to commence on 03 August 2026. Our Differences make our Performance At Air Liquide, we are committed to build a diverse and inclusive workplace that embraces the diversity of our employees, our customers, patients, community stakeholders and cultures across the world. We welcome and consider applications from all qualified applicants, regardless of their background. We strongly believe a diverse organization opens up opportunities for people to express their talent, both individually and collectively and it helps foster our ability to innovate by living our fundamentals, acting for our success and creating an engaging environment in a changing world. MANAGEMENT TRAINEE PROGRAM Program Management Trainee di PT SPJT (Perseroda) merupakan program pengembangan karier yang dirancang untuk mencetak talenta muda berpotensi menjadi calon pemimpin perusahaan di masa mendatang. Peserta akan mendapatkan pembekalan menyeluruh melalui pelatihan intensif dan rotasi kerja di berbagai unit, seperti konstruksi, keuangan dan akuntansi, sumber daya manusia, audit, pemasaran, administrasi proyek, hukum, teknologi informasi, hingga pengelolaan proyek. Melalui proses ini, peserta diharapkan mampu memahami alur bisnis perusahaan secara komprehensif, mengenal produk dan layanan perusahaan, serta memahami praktik manajemen yang diterapkan dalam operasional perusahaan. Selama program berlangsung, peserta akan terlibat langsung dalam berbagai aktivitas operasional dan strategis perusahaan. Tanggung jawab peserta mencakup membantu koordinasi proyek, melakukan analisis data dan penyusunan laporan, mendukung pengelolaan administrasi, mempelajari kebutuhan pelanggan, serta berkontribusi dalam upaya peningkatan efektivitas proses kerja. Peserta juga akan bekerja sama dengan para manajer dan tim lintas divisi untuk mengembangkan solusi dan strategi yang dapat meningkatkan efisiensi, produktivitas, serta kinerja perusahaan secara keseluruhan. Selain mendapatkan pengalaman kerja praktis, peserta juga diwajibkan aktif mengikuti berbagai sesi pelatihan, pengembangan kompetensi, dan kegiatan kepemimpinan yang telah dirancang perusahaan. Dalam setiap tahapan program, peserta diharapkan menunjukkan semangat belajar yang tinggi, kemampuan beradaptasi, serta inisiatif dalam memberikan gagasan dan inovasi yang mendukung kemajuan bisnis. Setelah menyelesaikan program, peserta akan dipersiapkan untuk menempati posisi strategis sesuai dengan kompetensi, potensi, dan kebutuhan perusahaan. Kualifikasi: Minimal Gelar Sarjana (S1) dibidang Ekonomi (Akuntansi/ Manajemen), Psikologi, Hukum, Teknik (Sipil/ Arsitektur/ Tata Kota/ Elektro/ Mesin), Teknik (Industri/ Pangan), Manajemen Pemasaran, Teknik Informatika/ Sistem Informasi/ Teknik Komputer/ Ilmu Komputer/ Disiplin Ilmu lainnya dengan pengalaman Relevan. Min Diploma 3 (Analis Kimia/ Teknik Kimia/ Kimia Industri), IPK Min 2,90 dari skala 4,00 Fresh Graduate / pengalaman dipersilahkan melamar IPK Min 2,9 (S1) dan 3,00 (S2) dari skala 4 Mahir dalam Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) Bersedia ditugaskan ke semua Unit Bisnis Perusahaan; Potensi Jiwa pemimpin, dengan pola pikir proaktf dan berorientasi pada hasil; Komunikasif dan memiliki jiwa Interpersonal yang kuat Kemampuan berpikir kritis dan analitis Mudah beradaptasi dengan lingkungan baru, Tangguh terhadap tekanan, dan bersedia ditugaskan ke luar kota/overtime. job_title: Impact Monitoring and Evaluation Senior Officer
job_description: Listed more than four days ago Impact Monitoring and Evaluation Senior Officer di Yayasan Hutan Tropis Ini adalah lowongan kerja Kontrak/Temporer Semarang , Jawa Tengah ( Hibrid ) Have a meaningful impact, with a desire to innovate and act for the environment Flexible working arrangement The opportunity to grow Have a meaningful impact, with a desire to innovate and act for the environment Flexible working arrangement The opportunity to grow Design and review of M&E frameworks, including indicators, baselines, targets, and data sources. Develop and produce narrative and analytical reports Design and review of M&E frameworks, including indicators, baselines, targets, and data sources. Develop and produce narrative and analytical reports subClassification: Analis Analis classification: Konsultasi & Strategi (Konsultasi & Strategi) 4 hari yang lalu 4 hari yang lalu About Earthworm With most of our staff operating directly on the ground where the issues are, we work with our members and partners to make value chains an engine of prosperity for communities and ecosystems. We see a world where forests are a boundless source of materials and a home for biodiversity; communities see their rights respected and have opportunities to develop; workers are seen as productive partners, and agriculture becomes the instrument to feed a hungry planet and keep our climate stable. Yayasan Hutan Tropis was legally established in Indonesia in 2005 as a national non-profit organization (Yayasan). We are affiliated with the Earthworm Foundation as the implementing partner for activities in Indonesia. Our offices are based in Semarang and Jakarta, with a total of 73 staffs. Our operational areas include Aceh, North Sumatra, Riau, and East Kalimantan. About the Landscape Program Protecting fragile ecosystems means finding a way to create value for people without sacrificing nature. This is too complex a challenge for any one company, commodity supply chain, NGO, or government to handle alone. To secure a future for natural resources and the people who rely on them, collective action and collaboration are critical – we must go beyond the bounds of individual supply chains. This is why we work in high-value landscapes across the globe to support local and international stakeholders in constructing pathways for regenerating lives and nature, building supply chain and farmer resilience, and achieving the goals of responsible sourcing. Your Role Being Impact Monitoring and Evaluation Senior Officer , your day will be primarily focused on: · Design and review of M&E frameworks, including indicators, baselines, targets, and data sources. · Provide technical M&E support to project teams, including guidance on tools, data collection, and reporting. · Coordinate with and guide internal teams and collaborate with partners to ensure alignment of data, reporting, and thematic approaches, while maintaining accurate, up-to-date, and accessible data through regular review and database management. · Analyze project progress against outputs, outcomes, milestones, and targets based on Theory of Change, log frames, and program objectives. · Develop and produce narrative and analytical reports (quarterly and annual) for Landscape programs and assigned projects. · Capture and document lessons learned from project implementation to support knowledge management and continuous improvement. · Support the Strategic Thematic & Impact Lead and other team members in developing impact measurement approaches. Your Profile Monitoring & Evaluation professional with technical capacity in designing and implementing results-based M&E systems within agricultural supply chains, natural resource management, and landscape programs. Experienced in developing and operationalizing Theory of Change (ToC), log frames, and indicator frameworks aligned with donor requirements and international standards. Proficient in establishing monitoring systems & plan, KPIs, and impact measurement methodologies, including outcome tracking, and evaluation design (qualitative and quantitative). Having collaborative mindset, with strong cross-functional coordination skills, capable of working across thematic, operational, and business development teams. Adept at producing high-quality technical reports, donor updates, and analytical briefs, ensuring consistency, accuracy, and alignment with programs objectives. Highly organized, proactive and detail-oriented, with strong ownership, excellent time management and prioritization skills. Brings a strong capacity to synthesize large volumes of data into clear narratives that support stakeholder engagement, programs accountability, and continuous improvement. Last, you will have a strong willingness to travel to remote areas for extensive period when needed. Working Conditions: Bachelor's degree in social or environmental science or any other relevant area is preferred 3 years of experience at minimum Native Bahasa Indonesia speaker; fluent in English with strong written and verbal communication skills. Full-time, Fixed-term (1 year) contract (possibilities of renewal) Semarang, Indonesia, office based with regular travels to the field (outside office hours) Indonesia national or valid permit required Joining us If you are in search of having a meaningful impact, with a desire to innovate and act for the environment, this offer is for you! The team you would join is truly committed to and passionate about finding concrete solutions to improve relationship between People and Nature. To join this adventure, apply now. Only selected candidates will be contacted. Good Luck!

job_title: Associate Product Manager - Subs Growth and Lifecycle
job_description: Listed more than three days ago Associate Product Manager - Subs Growth and Lifecycle di The Economist Group (Asia/Pacific) Limited Ini adalah lowongan kerja Full time Bandung , Jawa Barat ( Hibrid ) Associate PM driving subscriber growth via lifecycle optimization, monetization experiments, and data-led improvements across web and app journeys. Associate PM driving subscriber growth via lifecycle optimization, monetization experiments, and data-led improvements across web and app journeys. subClassification: Manajemen & Pengembangan Produk Manajemen & Pengembangan Produk classification: Pemasaran & Komunikasi (Pemasaran & Komunikasi) 3 hari yang lalu 3 hari yang lalu The Economist Group is the leading source of analysis on international business and world affairs. We deliver world-class, thought-provoking content through a range of formats, from web and app to print, conferences, film, and audio. What ties us together is the objectivity of our opinion, the originality of our insight, and our advocacy of economic and political freedom around the world. As our digital subscription business grows, so does the importance of delivering thoughtful, frictionless, and value-driven experiences across the entire subscriber lifecycle. About the role We are seeking a motivated Associate Product Manager to join our Subscriber Growth & Lifecycle product pillar. In this role, you will support the continuous improvement of subscription journeys that maximise subscriber lifetime value and foster long-term loyalty across web and app. This is a hands-on role with strong exposure to strategy and delivery. You will contribute to initiatives across retention, upgrades, and customer care, working closely with experienced product managers, engineers, analysts, marketers, and customer operations teams. It offers a valuable opportunity to develop deep expertise in subscription products, monetisation, and experimentation within a globally recognised media organisation. What you’ll do As the Associate Product Manager for Subscriber Growth & Lifecycle, you will be responsible for the following with support from more senior Product Managers: Own and optimise the end-to-end subscriber journey: You will be the product manager for key in-life subscription experiences, including upgrades, downgrades, cancellations, and re-engagement flows. You will continuously identify and address friction points, and champion a subscriber-first mindset in everything you do. Drive monetisation and experimentation: You will develop and execute a roadmap of monetisation initiatives, including offers, add-ons, and subscription tiers. You will design and run experiments to optimise our upgrade and retention paywalls, and use data to inform our pricing and packaging strategies. Innovate in customer care: You will partner with our Customer Care teams to redefine how we serve our subscribers. You will lead the development of our self-service and assisted service journeys, including implementing AI-powered bots and other automation technologies to improve efficiency and customer satisfaction. Champion a data-driven culture: You will define and analyse key metrics such as churn, upgrades, engagement, and customer satisfaction. You will use this data to measure the impact of your initiatives, identify new opportunities, and communicate your progress to stakeholders. Lead with influence: You will write clear and compelling user stories, acceptance criteria, and product documentation. You will use your strong communication and collaboration skills to align your team and stakeholders around a shared vision and roadmap. What you’ll bring We are looking for a candidate who is passionate about building great products and eager to learn and grow. The ideal candidate will have: A proven track record of driving results: You have at least 2 years of experience in product management or a similar role (such as Product Marketing or Product Analytics) with a focus on digital customer journeys. You can point to specific examples of how you have used offers, discounts, add-ons, or subscription tiers to achieve business goals. A deep understanding of the subscriber lifecycle: You have experience with paywall campaigns, experiments, or launches across web and app. You are familiar with the key levers for driving subscriber engagement, retention, and lifetime value. A strategic and analytical mindset: You are comfortable working in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment with shifting priorities. You have a structured, data-informed approach to problem-solving and prioritisation. You are proficient in using data to make decisions and measure success. Exceptional collaboration and communication skills: You are a natural collaborator who can work effectively with designers, engineers, analysts, and operational teams. You can articulate your ideas clearly and persuasively, and you are a great listener. A "go-getter" mindset with high autonomy: You are a self-starter who takes initiative and can run with things independently. While you will have support from senior PMs, you are comfortable taking the lead on projects, navigating ambiguity, and proactively pushing initiatives to completion. Nice to have: Experience managing monetisation for a B2C app and web product. Exposure to monetisation automation, personalisation, or machine-learning-driven decisioning. Familiarity with subscription platforms or lifecycle marketing tools (e.g., Zuora, SF). Experience with data analysis and visualisation tools (e.g., Amplitude, Tableau, SQL). Why this role matters This is an opportunity to make a real impact on The Economist's future. By improving our in-life subscription experience, you will not only drive revenue growth but also help us build a more sustainable and resilient business. You will be at the heart of our efforts to create a lifelong relationship with our readers and to ensure that The Economist remains the world's leading source of analysis on international business and world affairs for generations to come. Location: This role is based in South East Asia .

Listed more than twenty one days ago Programme Policy Officer di World Food Programme Ini adalah lowongan kerja Full time Jakarta Pusat , Jakarta Raya Working with the World Food Programme Indonesia offers a unique opportunity to contribute to nationally scaled programmes. Working with the World Food Programme Indonesia offers a unique opportunity to contribute to nationally scaled programmes. subClassification: Kebijakan, Perencanaan & Regulasi Kebijakan, Perencanaan & Regulasi classification: Pemerintahan & Pertahanan (Pemerintahan & Pertahanan) 21 hari yang lalu 21 hari yang lalu JOB TITLE: Programme Policy Officer TYPE OF CONTRACT: National Officer (NOB) Fixed Term UNIT/DIVISION: Programme DUTY STATION (CITY, COUNTRY): Jakarta, Indonesia DURATION: 12 months BACKGROUND AND PURPOSE OF THE ASSIGNMENT: In January 2025, the Government of Indonesia launched the Makan Bergizi Gratis or Free Nutritious Meals Programme (FNMP), a transformative flagship programme to invest in the human capital of its young generation, supporting the Golden Indonesia 2045 vision and the goal of achieving 8 percent economic growth by 2029. It is designed to tackle multisectoral development challenges by improving nutrition, health and education, boosting local agricultural production, strengthening food systems and reducing poverty.​ The programme aims to reach 83 million beneficiaries by 2025, including infants, pregnant and breastfeeding group, and school-aged children. WFP is committed to supporting the Government of Indonesia, to enhance the sustainability, effectiveness and impact of the FNMP, by strengthening institutional capacities, providing tailored technical assistance and augmenting implementation capacities to achieve long-term improvements across education, nutrition and the local economy. ACCOUNTABILITIES/RESPONSIBILITIES: Under the overall supervision of the Head of School Meals, the Programme Policy Officer NOB will contribute to WFP Country Office efforts to support the government with the technical, operational and strategic implementation of the Free Nutritious Meals Programme, ensuring alignment with the national priorities and WFP standards. The NOB will carry out the following activities: 1. Manage implementation of technical assistance to the Government a. Lead the design and periodic revision of project workplans, ensuring they align with national strategies, WFP’s Country Strategic Plan (CSP), and donor expectations. b. Manage the provision of technical assistance to the Government effectively, ensuring responsiveness to emerging needs, alignment with national policies, priorities and strategic goals, as well as the technical expertise, operational modalities and strategic objectives of WFP. c. Assist government stakeholders in identifying context-appropriate, evidence-based and sustainable solutions to operational, technical or policy-related challenges encountered during the national programme scale-up and implementation. d. Provide support for the effective implementation of WFP’s initiatives contributing to the Free Nutritious Meals Programme. e. Lead technical support to facilitate the effective integration of fortified food into the Free Nutritious Meals Programme, through engagement with relevant ministries and technical partners (including fortification experts, food safety authorities, supply chain specialists, and academic institutions), as well as strengthening government capacity. f. Lead and coordinate the planning, implementation, and monitoring of Social and Behaviour Change Communication (SBCC) activities related to school and adolescent health and nutrition. g. Support data collection and monitoring to ensure high quality and evidence-based results feed into programme steering, reporting and decision making. 2. Facilitate external and internal coordination a. Lead effective coordination and engagement with relevant government counterparts at national and sub-national levels, including line ministries, technical agencies, and coordinating bodies and act as a focal point for coordination meetings, technical working groups and other collaborative platforms. b. Foster strong collaboration and information-sharing with other development partners, including UN agencies, international organizations, NGOs, and private sector actors, to promote harmonization of efforts, avoid duplication, and leverage complementary expertise and resources. c. Promote cross-sectoral coordination within WFP by actively engaging with relevant functional units to ensure an integrated and consistent approach in support of programme objectives in the Country Office and when required with the Global Headquarters of WFP to follow up on technical requests. 3. Manage partnership development and resource mobilization a. Proactively identify and explore opportunities for new partnerships and strategic collaborations with a broad range of stakeholders, including planning, finalization and operationalization of partnership agreements. b. Support WFP’s resource mobilization strategy by assisting in the preparation of donor engagement materials, including funding proposals, concept notes and other advocacy material. 4. Lead advocacy and knowledge management a. Lead the planning, coordination, and execution of key meetings, workshops, missions, and high-level events. b. Prepare high quality reports, minutes, advocacy messages, briefing materials, talking points, presentations and technical content to support analysis, discussions and decision making. 5. Budget monitoring a. Manage regular monitoring of programme budget to ensure that financial resources are utilized efficiently, in compliance with WFP’s financial rules and donor requirements, and aligned with approved work plans and strategic priorities. 6. Miscellaneous a. Act as Officer-in-Charge (OIC) for the Head of School Meals during periods of absence, assuming delegated responsibilities to ensure the continuity of leadership, coordination, and programme oversight. b. Perform any other related tasks as assigned by the supervisor. QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCE REQUIRED: Education: Advanced University degree in Public Policy, International Development, Nutrition, Education, or a related field. Experience: At least five years of progressively responsible professional experience in programme management, government coordination, or related areas. Previous work experience with school meals or with national sector platforms (in education, food security, nutrition, health, social protection, agriculture and/or related field). Experience in food fortification and/or SBCC is an added advantage. Knowledge and Skills: Relationship/partner management, project management, excellent writing, oral communication and analytical skills, coordination and communication, proposal writing, strategic thinking and proficiency in Microsoft Office. Languages: Fluency (level C) in English and Bahasa.

Do you enjoy meeting new people, building connections, and helping teams grow? We are looking for a Talent Acquisition Associate to join our Operations team! In this role, you will be the first point of contact for candidates joining our company. You will help us source, screen, and interview great people across our operational teams (GRO/Customer Service, Beautician, Store Leader, and more), and present the best ones to our hiring managers for selection. You will report directly to the Operational Manager, while also being part of our HR team. It is a fantastic opportunity to learn recruitment from day one, work closely with senior leaders, and grow into a future HR or People Operations professional. No recruitment experience? No problem! We will train you. What we care about most is your energy, curiosity, and willingness to learn. What You Will Do End-to-End Recruitment Source candidates through job portals (Jobstreet, LinkedIn), referrals, walk-ins, and job fairs. Screen CVs and conduct first-round interviews to assess candidate fit. Coordinate interview schedules between candidates and hiring managers. Present shortlisted candidates to the Operational Manager for selection. Recruitment Tracker & Reporting Maintain and update the Recruitment Tracker daily — every candidate, every stage, every status. This is a core part of the role. Prepare weekly recruitment reports: fulfilment rate, time-to-fill, sourcing channel performance, and pipeline health. Keep the candidate database clean, organized, and ready for the team to reference at any time. Candidate Pipeline & Sourcing Build and grow an active talent pipeline so we always have great candidates ready when business needs them. Explore creative sourcing channels — social media, communities, campus networks, and beyond. Provide a warm and professional candidate experience throughout the hiring journey. Handover & Coordination Work closely with the Operational Manager to understand hiring needs and timelines. Hand over endorsed candidates smoothly to the People Operations team for offering and onboarding. Flag blockers or changes early so the team can move quickly. Job Posting & Employer Branding Post job openings on Jobstreet, LinkedIn, Karir.com, and other relevant platforms. Help keep our employer branding fresh, friendly, and aligned with our company image. Track applicant numbers per posting and suggest improvements. Who We Are Looking For Education Fresh graduates or final-year students (D3/S1) from Psychology, Human Resource Management, Management, Communications, or related majors are encouraged to apply. Internship experience in HR, Recruitment, or People Operations is a plus — but not required. Skills & Mindset Eager to learn: you are curious, ask great questions, and pick things up quickly. People person: you enjoy talking to candidates and can build rapport easily. Organized & detail-oriented: you keep trackers up to date and never let candidates slip through the cracks. Driven & resilient: you can handle volume, deadlines, and a fast-paced environment. Communicative: you can write professional messages and follow up clearly with candidates and Users. Adaptive: comfortable when priorities or hiring targets shift. Technical Knowledge Comfortable with Microsoft Excel (basic to intermediate) and Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets). Familiar with AI tools and able to use AI for real work — drafting job ads, screening notes, candidate communications, and speeding up daily tasks. Familiar with social media and digital platforms; bonus if you have used LinkedIn, Jobstreet, or Karir.com before. Basic understanding of Indonesian Labor Law (UU Ketenagakerjaan) is a plus — we will train you. WhatsApp Business knowledge is a bonus for high-volume candidate communication. Languages Bahasa Indonesia (fluent / native). Basic English — enough to read job descriptions and write simple messages. Other Able to stay calm and focused during peak hiring seasons. What You Will Get A structured learning path — you will learn recruitment end-to-end from sourcing to handover. Mentorship from experienced Operations and People Operations leaders. Exposure to high-volume, real-world hiring across multiple operational roles. A clear career path into Recruitment Specialist, HR Generalist, or People Operations roles. A collaborative team that values speed, ownership, and kindness.

Sarana Pembangunan Jawa Tengah (Perseroda) merupakan Badan Usaha Milik Daerah (BUMD) milik Pemerintah Provinsi Jawa Tengah yang bergerak di bidang infrastruktur, manufaktur, dan jasa konstruksi. Sebagai perusahaan daerah yang memiliki peran strategis dalam mendukung pembangunan dan pertumbuhan ekonomi, SPJT berkomitmen memberikan kontribusi nyata bagi perkembangan perekonomian daerah melalui pengembangan wilayah, penguatan permodalan, peningkatan pendapatan daerah, serta optimalisasi kinerja perusahaan guna memperoleh laba dan manfaat berkelanjutan. Sejalan dengan visi tersebut, SPJT membuka kesempatan bagi generasi muda terbaik Indonesia untuk bergabung melalui Program Management Trainee . MANAGEMENT TRAINEE PROGRAM Program Management Trainee di PT SPJT (Perseroda) merupakan program pengembangan karier yang dirancang untuk mencetak talenta muda berpotensi menjadi calon pemimpin perusahaan di masa mendatang. Peserta akan mendapatkan pembekalan menyeluruh melalui pelatihan intensif dan rotasi kerja di berbagai unit, seperti konstruksi, keuangan dan akuntansi, sumber daya manusia, audit, pemasaran, administrasi proyek, hukum, teknologi informasi, hingga pengelolaan proyek. Melalui proses ini, peserta diharapkan mampu memahami alur bisnis perusahaan secara komprehensif, mengenal produk dan layanan perusahaan, serta memahami praktik manajemen yang diterapkan dalam operasional perusahaan. Selama program berlangsung, peserta akan terlibat langsung dalam berbagai aktivitas operasional dan strategis perusahaan. Tanggung jawab peserta mencakup membantu koordinasi proyek, melakukan analisis data dan penyusunan laporan, mendukung pengelolaan administrasi, mempelajari kebutuhan pelanggan, serta berkontribusi dalam upaya peningkatan efektivitas proses kerja. Peserta juga akan bekerja sama dengan para manajer dan tim lintas divisi untuk mengembangkan solusi dan strategi yang dapat meningkatkan efisiensi, produktivitas, serta kinerja perusahaan secara keseluruhan. Selain mendapatkan pengalaman kerja praktis, peserta juga diwajibkan aktif mengikuti berbagai sesi pelatihan, pengembangan kompetensi, dan kegiatan kepemimpinan yang telah dirancang perusahaan. Dalam setiap tahapan program, peserta diharapkan menunjukkan semangat belajar yang tinggi, kemampuan beradaptasi, serta inisiatif dalam memberikan gagasan dan inovasi yang mendukung kemajuan bisnis. Setelah menyelesaikan program, peserta akan dipersiapkan untuk menempati posisi strategis sesuai dengan kompetensi, potensi, dan kebutuhan perusahaan. Sebagai perusahaan daerah yang memiliki peran strategis dalam mendukung pembangunan dan pertumbuhan ekonomi, SPJT berkomitmen memberikan kontribusi nyata bagi perkembangan perekonomian daerah melalui pengembangan wilayah, penguatan permodalan, peningkatan pendapatan daerah, serta optimalisasi kinerja perusahaan guna memperoleh laba dan manfaat berkelanjutan. Sejalan dengan visi tersebut, SPJT membuka kesempatan bagi generasi muda terbaik Indonesia untuk bergabung melalui Program Management Trainee . MANAGEMENT TRAINEE PROGRAM Program Management Trainee di PT SPJT (Perseroda) merupakan program pengembangan karier yang dirancang untuk mencetak talenta muda berpotensi menjadi calon pemimpin perusahaan di masa mendatang. Peserta akan mendapatkan pembekalan menyeluruh melalui pelatihan intensif dan rotasi kerja di berbagai unit, seperti konstruksi, keuangan dan akuntansi, sumber daya manusia, audit, pemasaran, administrasi proyek, hukum, teknologi informasi, hingga pengelolaan proyek. Melalui proses ini, peserta diharapkan mampu memahami alur bisnis perusahaan secara komprehensif, mengenal produk dan layanan perusahaan, serta memahami praktik manajemen yang diterapkan dalam operasional perusahaan. Selama program berlangsung, peserta akan terlibat langsung dalam berbagai aktivitas operasional dan strategis perusahaan. Tanggung jawab peserta mencakup membantu koordinasi proyek, melakukan analisis data dan penyusunan laporan, mendukung pengelolaan administrasi, mempelajari kebutuhan pelanggan, serta berkontribusi dalam upaya peningkatan efektivitas proses kerja. Peserta juga akan bekerja sama dengan para manajer dan tim lintas divisi untuk mengembangkan solusi dan strategi yang dapat meningkatkan efisiensi, produktivitas, serta kinerja perusahaan secara keseluruhan. Selain mendapatkan pengalaman kerja praktis, peserta juga diwajibkan aktif mengikuti berbagai sesi pelatihan, pengembangan kompetensi, dan kegiatan kepemimpinan yang telah dirancang perusahaan. Dalam setiap tahapan program, peserta diharapkan menunjukkan semangat belajar yang tinggi, kemampuan beradaptasi, serta inisiatif dalam memberikan gagasan dan inovasi yang mendukung kemajuan bisnis. Setelah menyelesaikan program, peserta akan dipersiapkan untuk menempati posisi strategis sesuai dengan kompetensi, potensi, dan kebutuhan perusahaan.
